item.title: Peripheral Immune Challenge Drives Enteric α-Synuclein and Tau Pathology in LRRK2 G2019S Mice.
item.description: Aging and disease - Parkinson's disease (PD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by α-synuclein aggregation and dopaminergic neuron loss. Non-motor symptoms, including gastrointestinal dysfunctio...

item.title: Stress-induced IL-6 regulation in pediatric bone growth disorders: current insights and therapeutic strategies.
item.description: Frontiers in endocrinology - Review - Stress-induced upregulation of interleukin-6 (IL-6) signaling and its downstream pathophysiological consequences have garnered considerable attention in recent years. However, no comprehensive revi...

item.title: Intestinal spirochetosis in the modern era: a decade-long clinicopathologic study.
item.description: American journal of clinical pathology - Intestinal spirochetosis (IS) is infrequently identified in about 1% of human colonic biopsy specimens. On routine sections, it is diagnosed by identifying a thick band of basophilia on the apical ...
